tasks.title: Fix path traversal vulnerability in auth import/export
evidence: v1/auth/import and v1/auth/export take a raw 'path' from the request body
This allows reading/writing files anywhere on the disk the process has access to
plan: Modify handleAuthImport and handleAuthExport to validate the path
Restrict paths to a safe directory or use a predefined filename relative to DataDir
Consider removing absolute path support from these endpoints
notes: Fixed path traversal vulnerability in auth import/export
Added validateVaultPath to restrict paths to simple filenames
Paths now resolved relative to DATA_DIR only
Added comprehensive test coverage for path traversal attacks
Updated OpenAPI spec with path validation pattern

tasks.title: Implement RenderWaitModeNetworkIdle in ChromedpFetcher
evidence: ChromedpFetcher.performWait for RenderWaitModeNetworkIdle is a stub (2s sleep)
Robust network idle requires implementing event listeners for network.EventRequestPaused or similar
plan: Implement network activity tracking using chromedp listeners
Replace 2s sleep with a wait loop that checks for zero inflight requests
notes: Implemented network idle detection using chromedp event listeners
Replaced 2-second sleep stub with proper wait loop checking for zero inflight requests
Added comprehensive unit tests (7 test cases) and integration test
All tests pass including CI gate (generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test)
Fixed potential race conditions with atomic operations and channel double-close protection

tasks.title: Implement browser availability check

All tests pass and CI gate passes

tasks.title: Capture actual status in ChromedpFetcher

tasks.title: Optimize PlaywrightFetcher browser reuse
evidence: PlaywrightFetcher initializes a new Playwright and browser instance for every fetch

tasks.title: Implement pagination in Store.List

tasks.title: Cap HTTP fetch response size to prevent memory exhaustion
evidence: internal/fetch/http_fetcher.go reads response bodies with io.ReadAll(resp.Body) and no size limit, so very large responses can exhaust memory.
internal/config/config.go has no setting to cap response size for fetch operations.
plan: Add a configurable max response bytes setting in config + env (.env/.env.example) and plumb it into fetch.Request.
Enforce the limit in HTTPFetcher (limit reader / MaxBytes) and return a clear error when exceeded.
Add tests for over-limit responses and confirm normal-sized responses still succeed.
notes: Added configurable MaxResponseBytes field to config (env: MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES, default 10MB)
Enforced size limit in HTTPFetcher using io.LimitReader with +1 overflow detection
Plumbed MaxResponseBytes through all modules: scrape, research, crawl, jobs, cli, mcp, api
Added comprehensive tests in http_fetcher_test.go covering normal, at-limit, over-limit, and zero-limit (backward compat) cases

tasks.title: Stop MCP job manager on Close and use cancellable context
evidence: internal/mcp/server.go NewServer starts the manager with context.Background(), so workers never stop with server shutdown.

tasks.title: Close HTTP response body on error to avoid leaks

offset is 0-indexed: page=1 means offset=0, page=2 means offset=100, etc.

evidence: internal/api/server.go:210: handleScrape uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields, silently ignoring typos like 'ur' instead of 'url'.
internal/api/server.go:283: handleCrawl uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields.
internal/api/server.go:356: handleResearch uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req) without DisallowUnknownFields.

plan: Add decoder := json.NewDecoder(r.Body); decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() pattern to handleScrape at line 210.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleCrawl at line 283.
Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleResearch at line 356.

Add decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() to handleAuthImport, handleAuthExport, handleSchedules (POST) at lines 659, 690, 738.

notes: Go json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields() returns json.UnmarshalTypeError for unknown fields, which should trigger the existing error handling after each Decode call.

tasks.title: Verify generated TypeScript API types match Go API contracts
evidence: API contract is api/openapi.yaml and generates TS client via 'make generate' (hey-api openapi-ts)
Found specific type mismatches between OpenAPI spec and Go implementations:
- Job.createdAt/updatedAt are time.Time in Go (RFC3339) but nullable strings in TS spec - Go never nulls these, spec should make them required
- ResearchEvidence.simhash is uint64 in Go but number in TS - correct (TS uses number for uint64)
- CrawlState fields (ETag, LastModified, ContentHash, LastScraped) are non-nullable string/time.Time in Go but nullable in TS spec - actual Go serialization never nulls these, spec should match
- StatusResponse uses {'status': 'string'} in Go but spec calls it StatusResponse - correct
- Delete operations return {'status': 'canceled'} or {'status': 'deleted'} in Go but spec only shows StatusResponse - correct
- HealthResponse Components map has interface{} values in Go but ComponentStatus type in TS - Go stores ComponentStatus, should be type-safe
- No automated verification that generated types match actual API responses
Type mismatches cause runtime errors in web client
plan: Review current type mismatches identified in evidence section
Fix OpenAPI spec to match actual Go behavior: make Job.createdAt/updatedAt required non-nullable strings
Fix OpenAPI spec: make CrawlState fields non-nullable (ETag, LastModified, ContentHash, LastScraped)
Review HealthResponse Components type in Go - verify ComponentStatus is consistently used
Add integration test that calls API endpoints and validates responses against OpenAPI schema
Run 'make generate' to regenerate TS types after spec fixes
Verify web TypeScript compiles with new generated types ('cd web && pnpm run type-check')
Consider adding 'make verify-api' target that runs schema validation tests
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works after changes
notes: Fixed Job schema: made id/kind/status/createdAt/updatedAt required
Fixed CrawlState schema: removed nullable, made all fields required
Added format: date-time to Job.createdAt and updatedAt

tasks.title: Optimize job store performance with indexes on status and created_at
evidence: Store.init in internal/store/store.go creates 'jobs' and 'crawl_states' tables without any secondary indexes.
ListByStatus filters by 'status' and orders by 'created_at DESC', which requires a full table scan and sort.
ListOpts (used by List) orders by 'created_at DESC', also requiring a full scan for sorting.
ListCrawlStates orders by 'last_scraped DESC', which will degrade as the number of tracked URLs grows.
plan: Modify Store.init in internal/store/store.go to create indexes using 'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS'.
Add idx_jobs_status_created on jobs(status, created_at) to optimize filtered listings.
Add idx_jobs_created on jobs(created_at) to optimize default job listings.
Add idx_crawl_states_last_scraped on crawl_states(last_scraped) to optimize crawl state listings.
Verify index usage by running 'EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN' on the relevant queries in a test case.
Add a benchmark or performance test in internal/store/store_test.go to measure improvement with 10k+ records.
notes: SQLite indexes are bidirectional, but 'DESC' can be specified if we want to be explicit.
Composite index on (status, created_at) is most efficient for ListByStatus.
Added idx_jobs_status_created on jobs(status, created_at DESC) to optimize filtered job listings.
Added idx_jobs_created on jobs(created_at DESC) to optimize default job listings.

tasks.title: Implement browser health monitoring and recovery for PlaywrightFetcher
evidence: PlaywrightFetcher shares a single browser instance across all calls via its internal state
ensureInitialized() returns nil immediately if f.initialized is true, without checking if the browser process is still alive
If the browser process dies (e.g. OOM), all subsequent fetches will fail until the process is restarted
No current mechanism exists to detect a hung or crashed browser instance during the fetch lifecycle
plan: Implement a health check using f.browser.IsConnected() and potentially a simple page operation in ensureInitialized
Add automatic recovery logic in ensureInitialized to call f.cleanup() and re-initialize if the browser is unhealthy
Add a context-aware timeout to the recovery process to prevent hanging on re-initialization
Add a test case in playwright_fetcher_test.go that simulates a browser crash (e.g. by closing the browser manually) and verifies recovery
notes: The health check should be lightweight to avoid significant latency on every fetch

tasks.title: Align JobSpec.Validate with validate.ValidateJob semantics
evidence: internal/jobs/spec.go:58-60 JobSpec.Validate() rejects TimeoutSeconds <= 0 and does not validate MaxDepth/MaxPages/URLs beyond presence.
internal/validate/validate.go:54-62 ValidateTimeout() allows 0 (means default), validates 5-300 range if non-zero.
internal/validate/validate.go:64-82 ValidateMaxDepth/MaxPages allow 0 (means default), validate 1-10/1-10000 ranges if non-zero.
internal/validate/validate.go:25-40 ValidateURL() validates URL format (scheme, host, parse errors).
internal/cli/scrape/scrape.go:52-60,88-99 CLI runs validate.ValidateJob() before CreateJob(), so --timeout 0 passes validation but CreateJob() fails at JobSpec.Validate().
internal/cli/scrape/crawl.go:49-59,87-99 Same double-validation pattern as scrape (validate.ValidateJob then CreateJob -> JobSpec.Validate).
internal/cli/scrape/research.go:52-60,92-105 Same double-validation pattern as scrape/crawl.
internal/api/scrape.go:66-68 API handler defaults timeout<=0 to manager.DefaultTimeoutSeconds() before CreateJob(), working around the inconsistency.
internal/api/crawl.go:68, internal/api/research.go:65 Same timeout defaulting pattern as API scrape handler.
internal/jobs/helpers.go:104-119 toInt() helper treats <=0 as use fallback (consistent with 0=semantics).
internal/jobs/spec_test.go:42-51 Test expects zero timeout to fail (will need updating).
plan: Update JobSpec.Validate() to call validate package functions for consistency (ValidateURL, ValidateMaxDepth, ValidateMaxPages, ValidateTimeout).
Remove JobSpec.Validate()'s hard timeout>0 check to match validate.ValidateJob()'s 0= semantics.
Consider adding timeout defaulting logic in CLI commands (like API handlers do) or document that timeout=0 is invalid in CLI context.
Update internal/jobs/spec_test.go:42-51 test to expect zero timeout to be valid (or update to test new behavior).
Add tests covering invalid URLs, invalid MaxDepth (<1 or >10), invalid MaxPages (<1 or >10000), invalid timeout (<5 or >300 when non-zero).
notes: API handlers work around this issue by defaulting timeout<=0 to manager.DefaultTimeoutSeconds() before calling CreateJob().
CLI commands do NOT have this defaulting, so --timeout 0 passes validate.ValidateJob() but fails at JobSpec.Validate().
Internal helpers.go:toInt() already implements 0= semantics, suggesting JobSpec.Validate() should align with this pattern.
Updated JobSpec.Validate to use validate package functions for consistency
Removed hard timeout>0 check; now allows 0 (default) per validate semantics

tasks.title: Block path traversal in force-delete job artifacts
evidence: internal/api/jobs.go:80-84 handleJob only checks id != "" before calling store.DeleteWithArtifacts when ?force=true, with no validation of ID format.
internal/store/store_jobs.go:184-195 DeleteWithArtifacts runs os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(s.dataDir, "jobs", id)) without path sanitization, allowing directory traversal via ids like "../../../etc".
internal/store/store_jobs.go:177-179 Delete does not verify a row was removed (no rows affected check), so arbitrary ids can trigger artifact deletion even when no job exists.
plan: Add a shared job ID validation helper (UUID format or strict alphanumeric pattern) and reject invalid ids with apperrors.Validation in API handlers.
Harden DeleteWithArtifacts by verifying the job exists before deletion and ensuring the delete path stays under the data dir (use filepath.Clean + prefix check or safe join helper).
Add tests covering path traversal attempts (e.g., "../", "..\"), non-existent ids, and valid UUID deletion for force delete.
notes: Vulnerability confirmed: malicious ID like "../../../etc/passwd" would resolve outside data directory.
Job IDs appear to be UUIDs in practice - validation should enforce this format.
Added UUID validation for job IDs in API handlers (validateJobID in util.go)
Hardened DeleteWithArtifacts with job existence check and path traversal protection using filepath.Clean + prefix check
